title: CharacterGen: Efficient 3D Character Generation from Single Images with Multi-View Pose Canonicalization
titleZh: CharacterGen: 基于单图像的多视角姿态标准化高效3D角色生成

abstract.original: 本文提出了CharacterGen，一个从单图像高效生成3D角色的框架。该方法通过图像条件多视角扩散模型将输入姿态校准为标准形式，同时保留关键属性，并使用基于Transformer的稀疏视图重建模型从多视角图像创建详细的3D模型。作者还采用了纹理反向投影策略，并整理了一个动漫角色数据集。通过定性和定量实验评估，该方法在生成高质量3D角色方面表现出色，适用于绑定和动画等下游应用。
abstract.zh: We propose CharacterGen, an efficient pipeline that generates high-quality 3D character meshes in a canonical A-pose from a single input image. The method first uses an image-conditioned multi-view diffusion model to canonicalize arbitrary input poses into consistent multi-view A-pose images while preserving identity and appearance. A transformer-based, generalizable sparse-view reconstruction model then converts these multi-view images into detailed 3D geometry. Texture is refined via back-projection and Poisson blending. The authors also collect Anime3D, a dataset of 13,746 stylized characters rendered in multiple poses and views. Quantitative and qualitative evaluations show CharacterGen produces animatable, high-quality shapes and textures suitable for downstream rigging and animation.

method.zh: CharacterGen follows a two-stage pipeline: (1) Multi-view pose canonicalization and image synthesis — an image-conditioned multi-view diffusion model (enhanced by IDUNet) lifts a single arbitrary-pose input to multiple consistent A-pose images from several camera views (four orthographic views are used as a default). IDUNet extracts patch-level image features from the input to guide the multi-view UNet, improving local identity preservation and cross-view consistency. (2) Sparse-view 3D reconstruction and texture refinement — a transformer-based sparse-view reconstruction network consumes the canonical multi-view images to predict geometry and coarse texture; final texture resolution is improved by projective texture back-projection and Poisson blending. The pipeline is trained on Anime3D (13,746 stylized characters rendered in multiple poses/views) and is designed to produce animatable A-pose meshes quickly (generation under ~1 minute).

conclusion.original: ons. To better evaluate how using an A-pose character aids skeleton rigging and animation process, we rig two 3D character meshes generated by our CharacterGen and ImageDream [Wang and Shi 2023] and visualize the animated characters in Fig. 11. It can be observed that non-A-pose characters encounter severe mesh cohe- sion problem and the body structure is severely distorted while our A-posed character can be successfully animated. 5 LIMITATIONS AND DISCUSSION While our method can generate 3D characters from a single input image in an arbitrary pose, certain limitations still exist. For the four-view A-pose image generation step, our method may not retain enough information when the character is in an extreme pose or is rendered from a non-common viewpoint. A-posecharacteranimationNon-A-posecharacteranimation Fig. 11. We compare CharacterGen’s animated 3D characters with Image- Dream [Wang and Shi 2023] As for future works, the integration of additional non-photorealistic rendering (NPR) techniques into the texture refinement stage may further enhance the texture quality of the generated characters. Moreover, leveraging our trained multi-view UNet structure, it may be possible to incorporate the SDS optimization method to achieve 3D character generation with superior geometry quality. 6 CONCLUSIONS This paper proposes CharacterGen, a novel and efficient image- prompt 3D character generation framework. We compile a new multi-pose, stylized character dataset Anime3D to train our pipeline. Our designs include IDUNet, which extracts patch-level features from the input condition image to guide multi-view A-pose character image generation. Subsequently, we utilize a transformer-based network to reconstruct 3D character meshes and propose to utilize the texture back-projection
conclusion.zh: CharacterGen presents a novel and efficient image-prompt 3D character generation framework that canonicalizes arbitrary input poses into A-pose multi-view images and reconstructs animatable 3D characters. Key components include IDUNet for patch-level guidance in multi-view diffusion and a transformer-based sparse-view reconstruction network, plus texture back-projection for high-quality appearance. The authors release Anime3D to support training and evaluation. Generated characters are suitable for downstream rigging and animation and demonstrate advantages over non-canonicalized approaches. Limitations include degraded performance when the input is an extreme pose or uncommon viewpoint, and room for improved texture and geometry (e.g., integrating NPR in texture refinement or SDS-style optimization for geometry).

takeawaysZh: 通过图像条件的多视角扩散将单张任意姿态图像转为一致的 A 姿态多视角图，有效缓解自遮挡与姿态歧义问题。
提出 IDUNet，将 patch 级的外观线索注入多视角扩散模型，增强身份特征保留与跨视角一致性。
结合基于 Transformer 的稀疏视图重建与纹理反向投影 + 泊松融合，输出可绑定、可动画且纹理细节较好的 3D 网格。
